parentQuestion: Which cranial nerve controls most eye movements, pupil constriction, and lens ac...

scaffolds.q: The pupil can change size to control how much light enters the eye.
scaffolds.explain: The pupil constricts (gets smaller) in bright light and dilates (gets larger) in dim light. Let me show you the mechanism: this is controlled by muscles in the iris — sphincter pupillae to constrict, dilator pupillae to dilate. Automatic aperture control. Exquisite precision.

scaffolds.q: Multiple muscles are needed to move the eye in different directions.
scaffolds.explain: Six extraocular muscles control eye movement: up, down, left, right, and rotational movements. Different nerves control different muscles. it's a coordinated system. Break one component, and the whole performance falls apart. Look at what occurs:.
